rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ARIS GIANNAKAKIS SAVVIDIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/26. (No earlier availability.) Courtesy copies of new papers were not received by the assigned examiner by the 2/19/26 deadline; they were skimmed online in hopes of not having to continue this hearing. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Family Code Section 760 provides that "Except as otherwise provided by statute..." property acquired by a married person during marriage is community property. Family Code Section 770 provides that property acquired by a married person after marriage by gift, bequest, devise, or descent is separate property. This Spousal Property Petition does not address HOW the decedent acquired the assets in these accounts. If Petitioner is unable to provide copies of statements, how does she know the account numbers and how title to the accounts is held? A guardian ad litem must be appointed to represent the minor child, unless Petitioner can prove that the assets were not separate property. Deadline to provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/13/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IN THE MATTER OF THE BARBARA M. KEELEY AND ALICE J
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Regaeding Trust Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/08/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Notice does not appear to have been given to the Attorney General (Probate Code Section 17203). 2) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 3) Petitioner sets forth three different interpretations of the trust's ambiguous language. In a verified supplement to petition, please clarify petitioner's position on which interpretation petitioner believes to be the most accurate interpretation of settlor's intent, and why. Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 03/25/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA T. HESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/22/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The petition caption reflects that this is a petition for both Letters of Administration and for Letters of Special Administration. The Court cannot appoint both an administrator and a special administrator on one petition. (3) Please file a copy of Decedent's father's durable power of attorney empowering Lisa Walker to act as his agent. (4) Who is/was Decedent's mother and when did she die, if she is deceased? [LRSF 14.18 D.1.] (Siblings are not heirs if a parent survived.) (5) When did Decedent's spouse die? (6) Who were his parents and when did they die? (7) If the predeceased spouse's siblings are his heirs (successors-in-interest to a portion of this estate), they have not waived bond. (8) "Proposed Administrator" is not a RELATIONSHIP TO THE DECEDENT. One is either kin or a stranger. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 4/8/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM T. COX
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/13/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Erica Little is not listed in Item 8 and did not receive notice of the petition (Probate Code Section 8110(b)). 2) Relationship to decedent must be listed for all persons listed in Item 8. 3) If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs (LRSF 14.18.C.1). 4) Item 7b(2) is checked, but boxes (a) through (e) are blank. 5) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (San Francisco Local Rule 14.7.A). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings and a proposed order to this examiner no later than 03/30/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
